title: Paper-guided token-level reconstruction only

queries: graph token alignment local reconstruction supervision soft prompt llm
reconstructive graph instruction tuning token-level alignment graph-level compression

papers.title: Toward Graph-Tokenizing Large Language Models with Reconstructive Graph Instruction Tuning
papers.claim: Stronger graph-token alignment supervision is useful, but it need not force a single global reconstruction bottleneck for every downstream behavior.
papers.relevance: Supports keeping explicit local reconstruction pressure while relaxing the more brittle graph-level path.

papers.title: Can we Soft Prompt LLMs for Graph Learning Tasks?
papers.claim: The bottleneck is graph-text alignment quality, especially where graph content is converted into prompt tokens.
papers.relevance: Supports concentrating the next candidate on token-level alignment rather than stronger global graph compression.

papers.title: Graph-Aware Language Model Pre-Training on a Large Graph Corpus Can Help Multiple Graph Applications
papers.claim: Graph-aware supervision can help transfer, but the useful signal may depend on how local or global the auxiliary objective is.
papers.relevance: Supports a narrower supervision target after the broader graph-level reconstruction bundle regressed two representative tasks.

evidence.why_now: EXP060 showed that stronger reconstruction supervision can materially help user-churn, but the added graph-level reconstruction term appears to overconstrain user-ltv and item-incoterms. The next paper-backed step is therefore to keep token-level reconstruction only.

hypothesis: Token-level reconstruction pressure may provide the useful local alignment signal without the cross-task regressions caused by graph-level reconstruction. Removing graph reconstruction while keeping token reconstruction is the cleanest next finetune-only test.
notes: This is a finetune-only follow-up to EXP060.
Keep the refreshed 512-step screening protocol unchanged.
Only the graph reconstruction auxiliary term is removed; the token reconstruction term stays active.
Launched on 2026-05-06 against the refreshed 512-step strict baselines on GPUs 5/6/7.
Completed on 2026-05-07 with global verdict: failed.
user-churn improved, but user-ltv and item-incoterms both regressed against the refreshed 512-step strict baselines.
